skill.name: high-end-visual-design
skill.description: Guides AI agents to create premium web interface designs with refined typography, spacing, motion, card systems, and mobile behavior.
skill.summary: Create high-end UI concepts with premium layout, motion, typography, and responsive design rules.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external commands, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from markdown inline code and design prose in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, subprocess calls, network activity, file access, cryptographic APIs, or system enumeration logic were found. A low-severity transparency concern remains because the skill asks the model to make some design choices silently.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to markdown inline code for fonts, CSS classes, Tailwind utilities, colors, and animation examples. I found no evidence of Ruby backtick execution, shell commands, subprocess invocation, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is documentation using markdown code formatting for design tokens and CSS utility names. There is no executable file or code path that could run shell commands.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto And Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts point to skill metadata and design instructions, not cryptographic code or host inspection behavior. I found no evidence of hashing APIs, encryption routines, uname-style inspection, environment probing, or data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain natural language and UI layout guidance only. They do not include APIs, commands, URLs, file paths, or logic associated with crypto weakness or reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Transparency Concern In Design Selection Instructions
low_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to silently choose a design archetype before producing code. This is not a prompt injection attempt, but hidden selection steps can reduce user visibility into why a particular visual direction was chosen.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and present in the skill text, but the impact is limited to transparency of creative process. It does not ask the model to ignore system instructions, skip review, or conceal harmful behavior.

content.value_statement: Many AI-generated interfaces look generic because they reuse default fonts, grids, spacing, and motion. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete visual rules for premium web design.
actual_capabilities: Defines premium typography, spacing, shadows, borders, cards, and motion rules for generated web interfaces.
Provides layout archetypes for bento grids, layered cascades, and editorial split screens.
Includes mobile collapse rules for asymmetric layouts and full-height sections.
Specifies motion guidance for navigation reveals, button hover states, and scroll entry animations.
Adds performance guardrails for transform-only animations, blur use, noise overlays, and z-index discipline.
limitations: It is a design instruction file, not a runtime framework or component library.
It assumes access to premium fonts and icon styles, which may require separate setup.
It favors expressive marketing and product surfaces over dense enterprise dashboards.
It does not include tests, assets, scripts, or reusable design tokens.

use_cases.title: Premium Landing Page Direction
use_cases.description: Generate a visually distinct landing page concept with refined spacing, typography, motion, and responsive behavior.

use_cases.title: Frontend Prototype Polish
use_cases.description: Improve an existing React or Tailwind prototype so it feels more deliberate, tactile, and agency-grade.

use_cases.title: Design System Inspiration
use_cases.description: Create consistent card, button, navigation, and animation patterns for a premium digital product.

prompt_templates.title: Upgrade A Simple Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for improving a basic screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the high-end visual design skill to redesign this page with premium typography, spacing, card structure, motion, and mobile behavior. Keep the content unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Landing Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a new marketing page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a premium landing page for this product. Choose a suitable visual archetype, create the main sections, and include responsive layout and motion details.

prompt_templates.title: Polish Existing UI Code
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for refining implementation quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React and Tailwind interface through the high-end visual design rules. Improve layout, cards, buttons, transitions, and mobile collapse behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Complete Visual System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a broader design direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a premium visual system for this web app, including typography scale, spacing rhythm, card architecture, navigation behavior, CTA patterns, motion rules, and responsive constraints.

output_examples.input: Redesign a generic SaaS homepage so it feels premium and modern.
output_examples.output: A refined interface direction with a floating glass navigation, asymmetrical feature layout, nested card surfaces, strong typography, and mobile-safe motion guidance.

output_examples.input: Improve a product card grid that looks too plain.
output_examples.output: A layered card system with outer shells, inner content panels, softer shadows, stronger whitespace, polished CTA treatments, and responsive single-column behavior.

output_examples.input: Create motion guidance for a portfolio page.
output_examples.output: A motion plan using staggered entry reveals, transform-only hover states, custom cubic-bezier timing, and restrained blur effects for fixed elements only.

best_practices: Use the skill with a clear product type, audience, brand tone, and page goal.
Ask for responsive behavior and performance constraints in the same prompt.
Review generated designs for accessibility, asset availability, and implementation cost.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for user research, accessibility review, or brand strategy.
Do not assume the named fonts or icon sets are installed in the project.
Do not apply heavy blur, motion, or decorative surfaces to dense operational tools without review.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps AI agents generate premium web interface concepts with stronger typography, spacing, card structure, motion, and responsive design.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown instruction file. It does not include scripts, packages, or runtime components.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found scanner false positives and one low-severity transparency concern, with no confirmed malicious behavior.

faq.question: What projects fit this skill best?
faq.answer: It fits landing pages, portfolios, SaaS interfaces, product pages, and visual prototypes that need a premium presentation.

faq.question: What should users verify after generation?
faq.answer: Users should verify accessibility, mobile layout, font availability, icon licensing, motion performance, and consistency with their brand.
